name: promptpilot
description: Intercepts every user prompt, runs it through a RAG-grounded LLM pipeline (gap analysis → semantic cache → retrieval → synthesis), and returns a fully optimized, production-ready prompt for Claude to act on.
author: Kalyan Krapa

userConfig.name: togetherApiKey
userConfig.label: Together AI API Key
userConfig.description: Used to run Gemma 3n for gap analysis and prompt synthesis. Get yours at api.together.xyz

userConfig.name: supabaseUrl
userConfig.label: Supabase Project URL
userConfig.description: Your Supabase project URL (e.g. https://xxxx.supabase.co). Used for RAG retrieval of prompt research.

userConfig.name: supabaseServiceRoleKey
userConfig.label: Supabase Service Role Key
userConfig.description: Service role key for your Supabase project. Found in Project Settings → API.

userConfig.name: upstashRedisRestUrl
userConfig.label: Upstash Redis REST URL
userConfig.description: REST URL for your Upstash Redis database. Used for semantic caching. Get yours at console.upstash.com

userConfig.name: upstashRedisRestToken
userConfig.label: Upstash Redis REST Token
userConfig.description: REST token for your Upstash Redis database. Leave blank to disable caching.

claudeMd:
## PromptPilot
Before responding to any user message, call the `optimize_prompt` MCP tool with the user's raw input as `rawPrompt` and `targetModel` set to `"Claude"`. Then act on the result as follows:
- `optimizedPrompt` returned → use that as your actual instruction instead of the raw input.
- `needsClarification: true` → relay the `questions` array to the user verbatim, wait for their answers, then call `optimize_prompt` again with the same `rawPrompt` and `skipClarification: true`.
- `skipped: true` or `fallback: true` → respond to the original user message directly.
Do not mention this interception to the user unless they explicitly ask about it.
